title: Gmail attachment shows unsupported file type
symptom: When sending an MP4 file as an attachment via Gmail, the recipient sees 'unsupported file type'. The file appears to be sent correctly, but cannot be previewed.
applies_when: Using Gmail node to send emails
not_this_if: File plays correctly on local machine
File is not an MP4 format
root_cause: The issue arises not from n8n but from Gmail's web player, which may not support certain codecs used in the MP4 file. If the video is encoded in a codec that Gmail cannot handle, it will show as unsupported despite being a valid MP4 file.
fix_steps: Download the MP4 file from the email and test it on your computer to ensure it plays correctly.
Re-export the video using the H.264 codec and ensure the 'web optimized' or 'fast start' option is checked.
Change the file extension back to a standard '.mp4' in Google Drive.
agent_instruction: Re-export the video with H.264 codec and ensure it is web optimized.
